title: 10 best herbal teas for bad breath
intro_desc: Herbal teas for bad breath are infusions made from various plants and spices that can help freshen breath and improve oral hygiene. These teas often contain antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Examples of herbal teas that are known to help with bad breath include peppermint, spearmint, sage, cinnamon, and clove. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals can experience fresher breath, improved digestion, and a reduction in the bacteria that cause bad breath. This can lead to increased confidence, better social interactions, and overall improved quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with bad breath because it contains menthol, a compound that has natural antibacterial and antiseptic properties. These properties can help kill the bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, peppermint tea can freshen your breath by leaving a cool, minty taste in your mouth. Furthermore, peppermint tea is a natural alternative to mouthwashes and breath mints that often contain alcohol and other chemicals that can dry out your mouth and actually make bad breath worse over time. Therefore, drinking peppermint tea can not only help treat bad breath, but also prevent it from occurring in the first place.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Peppermint tea contains antimicrobial compounds, such as menthol and limonene, that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath in the mouth.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint tea, due to rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, can help decrease gum inflammation and redness, which can contribute to bad breath.
Fresh breath: Peppermint tea's refreshing aroma, primarily from menthol, immediately masks bad breath odors and leaves a pleasant, long-lasting minty scent.
Salivation stimulation: Peppermint tea promotes saliva production, which helps wash away bacteria and food particles that cause bad breath.

Digestive aid: Peppermint tea can alleviate digestive issues like bloating, gas, and indigestion, which can indirectly improve bad breath by reducing the production of gases that contribute to halitosis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with bad breath because it contains antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. The tea's soothing qualities can also help alleviate any inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to halitosis. Furthermore, lemon balm is a natural breath freshener, leaving your mouth feeling clean and fresh. Drinking lemon balm tea regularly can help maintain good oral hygiene and keep bad breath at bay.

Thyme tea helps with bad breath because it contains powerful antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath. The main active ingredient in thyme is thymol, which has been shown to be effective at reducing oral bacteria and preventing plaque buildup. Additionally, thyme tea has a refreshing and aromatic flavor that can help to mask any lingering unpleasant odors. Drinking thyme tea regularly can help to maintain fresh breath and promote overall oral health.

Sage tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that help to eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. The tea's antioxidants also help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, sage has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help to kill off the germs that cause bad breath. Drinking sage tea can help to freshen breath naturally, while also providing soothing relief to a sore or irritated throat.

Oregano tea helps with bad breath because it contains compounds with antimicrobial properties, such as carvacrol and thymol. These compounds can help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, oregano tea can help to stimulate the production of saliva, which can help to wash away food particles and bacteria that can contribute to bad breath. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe a sore throat or inflamed gums, which can be a source of bad breath. Furthermore, oregano tea has a strong, aromatic flavor that can help to mask bad breath temporarily.

Rosemary tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is a common cause of bad breath. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce inflammation in the gums, providing additional relief from bad breath. Moreover, rosemary tea is a natural breath freshener, leaving a pleasant and long-lasting smell in the mouth. Its refreshing and invigorating taste makes it an excellent alternative to traditional mouthwashes and breath fresheners. Drinking rosemary tea regularly can help keep your breath fresh and your mouth healthy.

Cinnamon tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that combat the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. The main active component in cinnamon, called cinnamaldehyde, is responsible for its potent antibacterial effects. Furthermore, cinnamon tea's warm temperature and astringent properties can help increase saliva production, which naturally washes away bacteria and food particles that can lead to bad breath. Additionally, cinnamon's refreshing and aromatic scent can temporarily mask any lingering unpleasant odors, leaving your breath smelling fresh and clean.

Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with bad breath because it contains antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that fight against the bacteria causing bad breath. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which has high levels of eucalyptus oil. This oil has been shown to have antibacterial effects that can kill off the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce any swelling or irritation in the gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea regularly can help maintain fresh breath and promote overall oral health.

Spearmint tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that can kill the bacteria in your mouth responsible for causing bad breath. The menthol present in spearmint tea also provides a refreshing and cooling sensation, temporarily masking any unpleasant odors. Moreover, spearmint tea can stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria, further preventing bad breath. Regular consumption of spearmint tea can therefore promote oral hygiene and freshen your breath naturally.

Narrow-leaved paperbark tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath. The tea is made from the bark of the Melaleuca squamea tree, which is rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and tannins. These compounds work together to reduce the growth of odor-causing microorganisms in the mouth, thus freshening the breath. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties can help tighten and cleanse the gums, reducing the risk of gum disease and bad breath. Drinking narrow-leaved paperbark tea regularly can help maintain oral hygiene and promote fresh breath.
